title: Historical confidence audit — recent recommendations/trades vs new 0.60 hard floor
type: read-only — no code edits, no runtime edits, no .env changes, no DB writes, no process restarts
objective: Determine whether the new MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.60 hard floor is likely to materially reduce trade frequency by auditing recent recommendation/trade confidence values. Produce a factual confidence distribution and pass-rate estimate for recent activity.
question_to_answer: Would the new MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.60 gate have blocked most recent recommendations/trades, or are there enough recent signals at or above 0.60 for the bot to still trade normally?
allowed_files: Any local log files containing recent recommendation outputs with confidence values
Any local log files containing bridge/open-trade entries with confidence values
trades_sports.db — SELECT only, no writes — only if confidence is actually stored in the DB schema
runtime/state artifacts (e.g. state.json or equivalent) if confidence is preserved there
mlb_model recommendation logs or output files if they exist and contain confidence
sports_bot_v2 logs if they contain confidence-bearing recommendation or trade records

Code files — read-only — only the smallest set needed to locate where confidence is emitted or persisted (e.g. the log/emit call site, the DB column name). Do not read full files unless the confidence field cannot be found without it.
do_not_touch: Any production code file — no edits
Any process or runtime control file
Any file not needed to answer the confidence question
The DB — no INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, or schema changes
required_read_scope: Step 1 — find where confidence is logged or persisted: check sports_bot_v2 logs and mlb_model output files first. If not found, do a narrow grep for 'confidence' in log paths only.
Step 2 — extract the most recent N recommendation/trade records that contain confidence values.
Step 3 — if confidence is in trades_sports.db, run a SELECT query to get confidence column for recent rows.
Step 4 — produce the distribution. Do not expand scope beyond what is needed for steps 1–3.
preferred_evidence_sources_in_order: 1. Recent recommendation output files or logs containing confidence
2. Recent bridge/open-trade log entries containing confidence
3. trades_sports.db SELECT if confidence is stored there
4. Minimal code reading only to locate the confidence field/log call

1_sample_window.description: State the exact sample window analyzed
required_fields: exact time window covered (start timestamp — end timestamp)

2_confidence_distribution.description: Bin all observed confidence values from the sample

3_hard_floor_impact.description: Summarize the direct impact of a 0.60 floor on recent activity
required_fields: percent of recent recommendations/trades that would PASS a 0.60 floor
qualitative estimate: lightly reduced / moderately reduced / heavily reduced trade frequency
count of executed trades with confidence >= 0.60
count of executed trades with confidence < 0.60
list of last 10–25 executed trades with: trade_id or slug, confidence, side, outcome if available
if recommendation confidence and executed-trade confidence differ, explain why (e.g. bridge filtering, rounding, field mapping)
Is 0.60 a realistic floor given recent signal quality?
If not realistic: what floor would preserve reasonable trade frequency? (e.g. 0.55, 0.58)
Confidence in the conclusion: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW based on data quality and sample size

required_metadata_in_result: WHERE confidence is actually stored or logged (exact file path or DB column)
WHETHER the audit used recommendation history, trade history, or both — and which was more complete
WHETHER any confidence data was missing, unavailable, or had to be inferred
acceptance_criteria: read-only confirmed — no files modified, no DB writes
numerical distribution produced across all 6 bins
explicit PASS count and FAIL count against 0.60 stated
trade-specific list provided (at least 10 rows if available)
conclusion states whether 0.60 is realistic and offers alternative if not
rollback: N/A — read-only task. No changes made, nothing to roll back.
worker_notes: Do not do a broad repo sweep.
If you cannot find confidence in logs within 3–4 targeted lookups, check the DB schema for a confidence column and run a SELECT.
If confidence is not stored anywhere, say so explicitly and report what IS stored (e.g. only market_id and side, no confidence column).
Do not invent or estimate confidence values — only report what is actually in the data.
If sample size is small (< 10 records), note this clearly and adjust confidence in conclusion accordingly.
Write the result to 06_OUTBOX_FROM_WORKER only. Do not write anywhere else.
